orig.description: The user SHALL be able to configure the clock radio to wake them up at the intended alarm time.
The user SHALL be able to set the current time and read the displayed time from the clock radio.
The user SHALL be able to configure radio playback and listen to radio audio from an external transmitter.
The clock radio SHALL support explicit radio operating modes so the user can keep radio playback on, off, or automatically driven by the alarm.

usecase.description: The clock radio wakes the user at the configured alarm time.
usecase.pre-condition: The current time is available, alarm settings are configured, and the clock radio is powered.
usecase.main-event: The user enables the alarm and sets an alarm time.
The user selects the radio operating mode and optionally sets radio frequency and volume.
The clock maintains the current timestamp.
When the current time matches the configured alarm time in automatic mode, the alarm manager triggers the alarm.
The radio broadcasts audio output to the user.
usecase.post-condition: The user is alerted by the clock radio at the requested time.
usecase.alternate-event: If the alarm is disabled, the radio mode is not automatic, the alarm time is not reached, or radio audio is unavailable, the user is not woken by radio playback.

usecase.description: The user sets or reads the current time from the clock radio.
usecase.pre-condition: The clock radio is powered and the display function is available.
usecase.main-event: The user sets the current time.
The clock function updates the internal timestamp.
The display function presents the timestamp to the user.
usecase.post-condition: The clock radio shows the current time to the user.
usecase.alternate-event: If the current time is not set, the displayed time may be incorrect.

usecase.description: The user configures the radio and listens to received radio audio.
usecase.pre-condition: The radio transmitter emits radio waves and the clock radio is powered.
usecase.main-event: The user sets radio mode, frequency, and volume.
The radio subsystem receives and decodes radio waves.
The radio subsystem broadcasts radio sound to the user.
usecase.post-condition: The user hears radio audio at the configured settings.
usecase.alternate-event: If no usable radio signal is received, radio sound is unavailable to the user.

constraint.description: The clock radio SHALL accept current time input, maintain the current timestamp, and provide a timestamp display to the user.
constraint.rationale: The user needs to set the correct time and later read it back from the device display.

constraint.description: The clock radio SHALL accept alarm time and alarm on or off settings from the user and derive current alarm time and alarm activation data for alarm management.
constraint.rationale: The user needs to decide whether wake-up is enabled and when it should occur.

constraint.description: The clock radio SHALL represent time as a timestamp containing an hour in the range 0 to 23 and a minute in the range 0 to 59.
constraint.rationale: The user expects the device to store current time and alarm time in a normal daily clock format that is unambiguous for display and alarm triggering.

constraint.description: The clock radio SHALL trigger an alarm when the current time equals the configured alarm time while the system is in `Radio AUTO` mode.
constraint.rationale: The automatic mode exists so the device can remain quiet until the scheduled wake-up condition occurs.

constraint.description: In `Radio AUTO` mode, the ringing state SHALL return to silent after an alarm timeout of 59 minutes.
constraint.rationale: The wake-up function should not leave the device ringing indefinitely after the alarm has been triggered.

constraint.description: The clock radio SHALL receive radio waves from the external transmitter and decode them into radio signals using the current frequency setting.
constraint.rationale: The user expects the device to transform incoming radio transmission into an internal signal usable for playback.

constraint.description: The clock radio SHALL broadcast radio sound to the user from radio signals, alarm input, activation, and volume level.
constraint.rationale: The radio path and the alarm path both exist to produce audible output that the user can hear.

constraint.description: The clock radio SHALL support the operating modes `Radio OFF`, `Radio ON`, and `Radio AUTO`, with transitions triggered by the `radio off`, `radio on`, and `radio auto` events.
constraint.rationale: The owner wants explicit user-selectable radio behavior rather than a single fixed playback mode.

constraint.description: The clock radio SHALL accept radio on or off mode selection, frequency, and volume settings from the user and derive activation, current frequency, and volume level data for radio playback.
constraint.rationale: The user needs direct control over whether radio playback is active and how it should be tuned and heard.

behavior.idef0:
ctrl User out AlarmTime out AlarmOnOff
in AlarmTime in AlarmOnOff ctrl ClockRadioHMI out CurrentAlarmTime out AlarmActivation
in Timestamp in CurrentAlarmTime in AlarmActivation ctrl AlarmManager ctrl RadioAuto out Alarm
behavior.description: Capture alarm settings, derive current alarm data, and trigger the alarm when the current timestamp reaches the configured alarm time in automatic mode.

function.description: The user-facing alarm setting function provides alarm time and alarm activation selection.
The HMI derives current alarm time and alarm activation data for the alarm manager.
The alarm manager triggers the alarm when timestamp and alarm conditions are satisfied.

behavior.idef0:
ctrl User out CurrentTime
in CurrentTime ctrl ClockRadioHMI out NewCurrentTime
in NewCurrentTime ctrl Clock out Timestamp
in Timestamp ctrl ClockRadioHMI out TimestampDisplay
behavior.description: Provide current time settings, maintain the current timestamp, and display the current time to the user.

function.description: The user-facing clock setting function provides the current time input.
The HMI propagates current time settings to the clock logic.
The clock maintains the internal timestamp.
The display path converts the maintained timestamp into user-visible output.

behavior.idef0:
ctrl User out Frequency out Volume
ctrl User out RadioOnOff
in Frequency in Volume in RadioOnOff ctrl RadioHMI out CurrentFrequency out VolumeLevel out Activation
in RadioWaves in CurrentFrequency ctrl RadioMMI res RadioTransmitter out RadioSignals
in Alarm in RadioSignals in Activation in VolumeLevel ctrl Radio out RadioSound
behavior.description: Capture radio settings, decode radio waves, manage radio modes, and broadcast radio sound to the user.

function.description: The user-facing radio setting function provides tuning and volume input.
The user consumes radio sound and controls radio mode selection.
The HMI derives activation, current frequency, and volume level for the radio path.
The radio MMI decodes incoming radio waves into radio signals.
The radio component combines alarm, activation, radio signal, and volume inputs into radio sound.

behavior.idef0:
in CurrentTime ctrl User out Timestamp out TimestampDisplay
in AlarmTime in AlarmOnOff in Timestamp ctrl User ctrl RadioAuto out Alarm
in Frequency in Volume in RadioOnOff in RadioWaves in Alarm ctrl User ctrl RadioTransmitter out RadioSound
behavior.description: Operate the clock, alarm, and radio functions needed by the clock radio, using the logical functions identified in the Capella source model.

function.description: Coordinate the clock, alarm, and radio sub-behaviors for end-to-end clock radio operation.

verification.description: 1. Enable the alarm, set an alarm time, and place the radio in `Radio AUTO` mode. 1. Operate the clock until current time equals alarm time. 1. Confirm the alarm is triggered at that moment. 1. Continue operating the device and confirm the ringing state returns to silent after 59 minutes.

description.brief: Verify current time maintenance and display

verification.description: 1. Set the current time through the user interface. 1. Confirm the maintained timestamp updates to the requested time. 1. Confirm the displayed timestamp presented to the user matches the maintained timestamp.

1. Initialize the device and confirm the default radio mode is `Radio OFF`. 1. Trigger `radio on` and confirm the system enters `Radio ON`. 1. Trigger `radio auto` and confirm the system enters `Radio AUTO`. 1. Trigger `radio off` from `Radio ON` and `Radio AUTO` and confirm the system returns to `Radio OFF`.

description.brief: Verify radio setting, decode, and playback path

verification.description: 1. Set radio frequency, volume, and radio on or off selection through the user interface. 1. Provide a usable radio transmission to the device. 1. Confirm the device derives current frequency, activation, and volume level from the user settings. 1. Confirm the radio subsystem decodes radio waves into radio signals and broadcasts radio sound to the user.

1. Review the maintained timestamp data structure in the implementation. 1. Confirm the timestamp contains hour and minute fields. 1. Confirm hour is constrained to 0 through 23 and minute is constrained to 0 through 59.

architecture.uml:
left to right direction actor User node "Clock Radio" actor "Radio Transmitter" as TX
User -- "Clock Radio" --
User -- "Clock Radio" --
TX -- "Clock Radio" --
architecture.description: Top-level logical context for a radio receiver alarm clock.

interface.description: The user enters the alarm time and enables or disables the alarm.
interface.software: The interface SHALL accept alarm time and alarm enable input from the user.

interface.description: The clock radio provides audible output to the user.
interface.software: The interface SHALL provide audible radio or alarm output perceived by the user.

interface.description: The external transmitter provides a radio channel to the clock radio receiver.
interface.software: The interface SHALL convey received radio-frequency signal from the external transmitter into the receiver path.

architecture.uml:
left to right direction node Clock node "Alarm Controller" node "Radio Demodulator" node Speaker
Clock -- "Alarm Controller" --
"Alarm Controller" -- Speaker --
"Radio Demodulator" -- Speaker --
architecture.description: Simplified logical architecture for the receiver alarm clock.

interface.description: The clock provides timing information to the alarm controller.
interface.software: The interface SHALL communicate current time needed to compare against the configured alarm time.

interface.description: The alarm controller provides an alarm trigger to the speaker path.
interface.software: The interface SHALL communicate alarm activation to the audio output path when alarm conditions are met.

interface.description: The radio demodulator provides decoded audio to the speaker path.
interface.software: The interface SHALL communicate decoded radio audio from the radio demodulator to the speaker path.

architecture.uml:
left to right direction node "Alarm Controller" node "Radio Demodulator" node "Audio Amplifier" node Speaker
"Alarm Controller" -- "Audio Amplifier" --
"Radio Demodulator" -- "Audio Amplifier" --
"Audio Amplifier" -- Speaker --
architecture.description: Physical audio path showing how alarm and radio audio drive the speaker.

interface.description: A wire carries alarm trigger output from the alarm controller to the audio path.
interface.electrical: The alarm wire SHALL enable the audio path when alarm conditions are met.

interface.description: A wire carries decoded audio signal from the radio demodulator to the audio amplifier.
interface.electrical: The analog audio wire SHALL carry demodulated audio from the receiver stage to the amplifier stage.

interface.description: A wire carries amplified audio from the audio amplifier to the speaker.
interface.mechanical: The speaker SHALL be mounted to radiate audible sound from the enclosure.
interface.electrical: The speaker wire SHALL carry amplified audio current from the amplifier to the speaker.

architecture.uml:
left to right direction actor "Radio Transmitter" as TX node Antenna node "Radio Demodulator"
TX -- Antenna --
Antenna -- "Radio Demodulator" --
architecture.description: Physical RF path showing broadcast reception from the external transmitter into the receiver hardware.

interface.description: The antenna receives the external radio broadcast.
interface.electrical: The RF path SHALL convey received broadcast energy from the antenna into the receiver circuit.

interface.description: The antenna feeds RF signal into the radio demodulator.
interface.electrical: The RF path SHALL couple the received broadcast signal from the antenna into the demodulator circuitry.

architecture.uml:
left to right direction actor User node "Alarm Buttons" node "Real-Time Clock" node "Alarm Controller"
User -- "Alarm Buttons" --
"Real-Time Clock" -- "Alarm Controller" --
architecture.description: Physical timing path showing alarm entry and time comparison hardware.

interface.description: The user presses physical buttons to set or enable the alarm.
interface.mechanical: The alarm buttons SHALL be accessible from the front panel.
interface.electrical: The button contacts SHALL convey alarm-setting input into the control electronics.

interface.description: A wire carries timing data from the real-time clock to the alarm controller.
interface.electrical: The timing wire SHALL carry current time data from the clock circuit to the alarm controller.
